fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: North Carolina Pharmacist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacist? Here's whether your license transfers to North Carolina - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacist license reciprocity north carolina, how to transfer pharmacist license to north carolina, pharmacist compact north carolina, north carolina pharmacist endorsement application

license_types.title: RPh, PharmD
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacist in North Carolina.

steps.title: Create Account and Submit Online Application
steps.description: Create an account or log in to the NCBOP Licensing Gateway. Complete the online 'Pharmacist by Endorsement' application form in its entirety and pay the non-refundable $350 application fee.

steps.title: Complete NABP ELTP Process
steps.description: Register with the National Association of Boards of Pharmacy (NABP) and complete the Electronic Licensure Transfer Program (ELTP) application. NABP will verify your existing pharmacist license(s) and transmit the necessary information to the North Carolina Board of Pharmacy.

steps.title: Pass the North Carolina MPJE
steps.description: Register with NABP for the Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) specific to North Carolina. After receiving an Authorization to Test (ATT) from NABP, schedule and successfully pass the NC MPJE through Pearson VUE.

steps.title: Undergo Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Complete the required criminal background check (CBC) by following the specific instructions provided by the Board after your application submission, which will include fingerprinting.

steps.title: Await Board Review and Licensure
steps.description: The North Carolina Board of Pharmacy will review all submitted documentation, including your online application, NABP ELTP results, MPJE scores, and criminal background check. Once all requirements are successfully met and verified, your North Carolina pharmacist license will be issued.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NAPLEX and MPJE.

provisional_text: Some states offer an intern or provisional license for graduates actively accumulating required hours or waiting to take the NAPLEX.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

faqs.question: How much does a North Carolina pharmacist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies by state. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is the MPJE required in all states?
faqs.answer: Almost all states require passing their state-specific Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) to verify knowledge of local pharmacy laws.

faqs.question: Does North Carolina have reciprocity for pharmacists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacist practice in North Carolina. To work in North Carolina, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacist license to North Carolina?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in North Carolina while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacist in North Carolina
hero.dek: To apply for a Pharmacist license in North Carolina by endorsement, you must complete an application through the NCBOP Licensing Gateway and fulfill all state-specific requirements.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: To begin your application, navigate to the NCBOP Licensing Gateway. If you are a new user, click 'New User' to create an account by providing your personal information and establishing your login credentials. Once registered, log in to your account and select the 'Pharmacist by Endorsement' application type to start your application.
